title: 7 Days Out of Africa
description:
Samburu is the most popular reserve in northern Kenya. Wildlife viewing is good, and most big safari animals are easily spotted. The arid environment is home to a variety of north Kenya specials – both birds and mammals.

Samburu offers great wildlife viewing and four of the Big Five are present. Rhinos are absent, but big herds of elephant cross the reserve. Of the big cats, leopards are very rewarding with some habituated individuals giving high-quality sightings. Most interesting are the northern Kenya specials including beisa oryx, lesser kudu, reticulated giraffe, Grevy’s zebra and the odd-looking gerenuk. Other parks that you will sample the adventure are Lake Nakuru, Lake Naivasha & Africa’s leading national reserve. perhaps worth of mention here is the wildebeest migration that occurs between July & October annually.

Day 1. Nairobi Airport/CBD Hotel to Samburu National Park

description:

Depart for Tsavo East entering through Bachuma gate and spotting game enroute, you’ll reach Voi Safari Lodge in time for lunch. After lunch depart for Taita Hills Game Sanctuary and check-in at the lodge. Embark on an afternoon game drive which ends after sunset.

description:
Day 1. Nairobi Airport/CBD Hotel to Masai Mara National Reserve

Depart from Nairobi in the morning (07:30 am) and drive along the floor of the Rift Valley to Maasai Mara National Reserve arriving at the camp in the afternoon for lunch. Afternoon game drives followed by dinner and overnight at the Ashnil Mara Camp or similar

Day 2. Maasai Mara Full Day Full day spent exploring the park in search of the Big Five. You will have a picnic Lunch in the plains of the Mara enjoying the wilderness. After another game drive in the afternoon, return to your camp.Dinner and overnight at Ashnil Aruba Lodge

title: Samburu Trail
description:
This tour takes you to three most visited parks in Kenya which are popular due to its unique animals. They are: Maasai Mara, Nakuru and Samburu. This once-in-a -life-time must go tour, is a dream come true for wildlife enthusiast.

Day 1. Nairobi Airport/CBD Hotel to Masai Mara National Park

You will arrive in Nairobi as per your flight schedule. Met and greeted by our customer service team who will assist you with your luggage to the waiting Landcruiser. Met by your driver and the safari begins. Drive along the rift valley to the world famous Masai Mara game reserve where you have a greater opportunity to see majority of the animals due to its vast savannah grassland, forests, hills and water points. Check-in and lunch is served before you begin your afternoon game drive (16:00 – 18:30 Hrs). Dinner and Overnight at Oloshaiki Camp or similar.

title: 2 Days Tsavo East Satao Camp
description:
Day 1. Mombasa Coast Hotel/Port Tsavo East National Park

Pick-up from your Beach Hotel by Shuttle Bus and drive to park gate. Transfer into Landrover 4×4’s enjoying a game drive en-route to Satao Camp. Lunch at Satao Camp. Second game drive in the afternoon. Dinner and overnight stay SATAO CAMP.

Early morning game drive before breakfast. After breakfast drive to park gate. Transfer toAir conditioned vehicle for relaxing drive back to Mombasa. Drop off at your beach Hotel.

title: 2 Days Tsavo East National Park
description:
Depart from your hotel/port and drive to Tsavo East. Enter via Bachuma gate and view game enroute to Voi Safari Lodge for Lunch. Afternoon game drive in Tsavo East, home to lions, buffalo, zebra and giraffes. Overnight at Voi Safari Lodge –

After breakfast leave the park with a game viewing en-route. After approximately 3 Hrs drive along Nairobi/Mombasa Highway you are dropped at your beach hotel/Port.

title: 3 Days Tsavo & Amboseli National Park
description:
This tour is an excellent circuit and one of the tours taken by the early travellers. It includes a visit to Tsavo East, West and Amboseli with its Majestic Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa, wildlife viewing is exceptional.

Depart for Tsavo East entering through Bachuma gate and spotting game en-route, you’ll reach Voi Safari Lodge in time for lunch. Afternoon game drive. Dinner and Overnight: Voi Safari lodge.

After breakfast depart from Tsavo East and proceed game viewing through Tsavo West and continue to Amboseli National Park. This park is home to a wealth of wildlife. Arrive in time for lunch at the Kibo Safari Camp. Take a rest before heading off for an afternoon game-drive in the Park. Perhaps you can take a picture with a herd of elephants in front of the snow-capped Kilimanjaro. Dinner and Overnight: Kibo Safari Camp

title: 3 Days Tsavo East & West Parks
description:
This tour takes you to Tsavo East and West, one of the oldest and largest national parks in Kenya. Voi has a panoramic view of the Tsavo plains and Ngulia is a preferred lodge for bird watchers since independence and is frequently visited by a baited leopard. The plains game, predators, crocodiles in Mzima springs, the rhino sanctuary, Sheitani Lava flow etc form the highlights of the tour.

Day 01 – MOMBASA –TSAVO EASTEarly departure to Tsavo East for game drive. Lunch is at Voi Safari Lodge. The Park is a natural area of flat, dry plains, with thorny bushes and swampy marshland with abundance game animals. Afternoon game drive. Return for dinner and overnight at your Lodge.

Day 02 TSAVO EAST –TSAVO WESTAfter breakfast embark on game drive en-route to Tsavo West National Park. Lunch at Kilaguni / Ngulia Safari Lodge. Afternoon game drive which includes a visit to Mzima springs. Return for dinner and overnight at Kilaguni Serena/ Ngulia Safari Lodge.

title: 3 Days Tsavo Wilderness
description:
This tour takes you to Tsavo East and West, one of the oldest and largest National parks in Kenya. Ashnil Aruba Lodge is in the vicinity of the wildlife concentration area and Severin Safari Camp is an iconic camp in the middle of the Bush. The plains game, predators, crocodiles in Mzima springs, the rhino sanctuary, etc form the highlights of the tour.

Depart from your hotel and drive to Tsavo East. Enter via Bachuma gate and view game en-route to Ashnil Aruba Lodge for Lunch. After lunch drive to Tsavo West National Park. Afternoon game drive en-route to the camp. Overnight at Severin Safari Camp

After breakfast, morning game drive to Mzima Springs with the chance to see crocodiles, fishes and hippo from the underwater observatory tank. Back to the camp for hot Lunch. Afternoon game drive in the Ngulia Rhino Sanctuary. Overnight at Severin Safari Camp.

title: 4 Days Kilimanjaro Explorer
description:
This tour takes you to Tsavo East, West and Amboseli National Park. Roam through the breathtaking plains of Tsavo and Amboseli. Sighting the iconic African wildlife including the Big Five up close from a private jeep, customized for ideal game viewing. Not to be missed is the sight of the tallest mountain in Africa, Mount Kilimanjaro at 5895 Meters above sea level towering over Amboseli. A visit to the Maasai Village in Amboseli, dancing and jumping to their rhythmic songs is equally exciting.

Depart for Tsavo East entering through Bachuma gate and spotting game en-route, you’ll reach Ashnil Aruba Lodge in time for lunch. Afternoon game drive. Dinner and Overnight: Ashnil Aruba lodge Lodge

After breakfast, depart for Amboseli National Park. Situated at the foot of the majestic Mount Kilimanjaro, this park is home to a wealth of wildlife but is particularly known for its Herds of elephants. Arrive in time for Lunch at Oltukai Lodge. Afternoon game drive in the Park. Dinner and Overnight: Oltukai Lodge

title: 6 Days Cheetah Trail
description:
You are picked up from Moi Airport driven to the famous Tsavo East National park. This is home to the red elephants – so coloured due to wallowing in the red soil. The park is home to the Big Five as well. Mid-morning game drive in the park and arrive in time for Lunch. Afternoon at leisure or take optional nature walk, bird walk. At 16:00 Hrs embark on afternoon game drive in the park in such of more animals. Dinner and overnight at Ashnil Aruba lodge on full board or similar.

Day 2. Tsavo East National Park | Amboseli National Park

Early Breakfast. Drive through the park and exit through Manyani gate onto Mombasa-Nairobi highway heading to Amboseli National park. This park borders Mt. Kilimanjaro (the highest mountain in Africa which was in Kenya but given out by the queen as a present to former colonialists of Tanzania. Arrive in time for lunch at the Lodge. Afternoon game drive ( 16:00 hrs to 18:00 hrs) expecting to see some more animals. Dinner and overnight at Kibo Safari Camp or similar on full board.

title: 4 Days Taita Hills /Amboseli/Tsavo West
description:
Viewing wild animals from the lodges terrace is an exhilarating experience. A game viewing driving in Taita game sanctuary, Amboseli and Tsavo East can be very rewarding. See you in Amboseli!

Depart for Taita Hills game sanctuary and check-in at the lodge. Embark on mid-morning before returning for lunch. After lunch embark on an afternoon game drive which ends after sunset. Dinner and Overnight: Saltlick Safari lodge

After breakfast depart for Amboseli National Park. Situated at the foot of majestic Mount Kilimanjaro, this park is home to a wealth of wildlife but is particularly known for its elephants. Arrive in time for Lunch at Sentrim Amboseli. Afternoon game viewing drive in the Park. Dinner and Overnight: Sentrim Amboseli

title: 5 Days Kenya Explorer
description:
This Tour takes you to Tsavo East before making a stop-over in Nairobi on your way to Mara to *watch the greatest wildlife show on earth, the wildebeest migration, where 2 million wildebeest, zebras and gazelle that group together for protection, wander cyclically between Tanzania’s Serengeti and Masai Mara in Kenya, as the predators stay in wait. Visit the Masai village to experience their cultural life of this people. On the last leg of the journey, a first class night train or a flight takes you to your beach resort. *wildebeest migration happens between July and October, and January to March.

Drive to Tsavo East national Park, have a game drive enroute to Voi safari lodge for lunch. In the afternoon have a game drive before returning to the lodge. Dinner and overnight: Voi Safari Lodge

After breakfast depart for Nairobi. Lunch at Carnivore. Transfer to Kipepeo Hotel, booked on Half-board basis (Dinner and breakfast). Overnight: Kipepeo

title: 6 Days Wilderness Migration
description:
Upon arrival at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport you are met and transferred to the hotel. Overnight at Osoita Lodge, Nairobi.

Breakfast is served. Leaving Nairobi at 7 am, you head south towards Narok, gateway to the Maasai Mara and district headquarters of this part of Maasai land. You’ll travel across the “breadbasket” of Kenya, covered with acres of wheat and barley, you’ll reach your destination in time for lunch. Later, embark on an afternoon game drive till sunset. Dinner and overnight at Mara Fisi Camp

The whole day on game viewing drive with Picnic Lunches. Dinner and overnight at Mara Fisi Camp.

title: 5 Days Samburu Wildlife Reserve & Lake Nakuru Out of Africa Safari
description:
Samburu is the most popular reserve in northern Kenya. Wildlife viewing is good, and most big safari animals are easily spotted. The arid environment is home to a variety of north Kenya specials – both birds and mammals. Samburu offers great wildlife viewing and four of the Big Five are present. Rhinos are absent, but big herds of elephant cross the reserve. Of the big cats, leopards are very rewarding with some habituated individuals giving high-quality sightings. Most interesting are the northern Kenya specials including beisa oryx, lesser kudu, reticulated giraffe, Grevy’s zebra and the odd-looking gerenuk. Other parks that you will sample the adventure are Lake Nakuru, Lake & Africa’s leading national reserve – Masai Mara. Perhaps worth of mention here is the wildebeest migration that occurs between July & October annually.

Day 1. Nairobi Airport/CBD Hotel to Samburu National Park

Samburu is the most popular reserve in northern Kenya. Wildlife viewing is good, and most big safari animals are easily spotted. The arid environment is home to a variety of north Kenya specials – both birds and mammals. Samburu offers great wildlife viewing and four of the Big Five are present. Rhinos are absent, but big herds of elephant cross the reserve. Of the big cats, leopards are very rewarding with some habituated individuals giving high-quality sightings. Most interesting are the northern Kenya specials including beisa oryx, lesser kudu, reticulated giraffe, Grevy’s zebra and the odd-looking gerenuk.

title: Three-Day Tarangire & Ngorongoro Crater Safari
description: Tarangire National Park is known for its large elephant herds and iconic baobab trees, while Ngorongoro Crater offers a natural wildlife sanctuary with breathtaking views and diverse animal species.

title: Four-Day Serengeti & Ngorongoro Safari
description:
The Serengeti is famous for the Great Migration, vast plains, and abundant predators, while Ngorongoro Crater is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with a dense concentration of wildlife.

ItineraryDay 1
– Depart from Arusha at 7:00 AM.
– Arrive at Serengeti National Park by 12:00 PM.
– Lunch at the lodge.
– Afternoon game drive at 3:00 PM.
– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** in Serengeti.

Day 2
– Full-day game drive in Serengeti.
– Picnic lunch in the park.
– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** in Serengeti.

title: Five-Day Tarangire, Serengeti & Ngorongoro Safari
description:
This itinerary covers three of Tanzania’s most famous parks, offering a **diverse safari experience** with elephants, lions, and breathtaking landscapes.

ItineraryDay 1– Depart from Arusha at 7:00 AM.– Arrive at Tarangire National Park by 9:00 AM for a game drive.– Lunch at a picnic site inside the park.– Afternoon game drive at 3:00 PM.– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** near Tarangire.

Day 2
– Early morning game drive at 6:30 AM.
– Breakfast at 8:30 AM.
– Depart for Serengeti at 10:00 AM.
– Lunch at the lodge at 12:30 PM.
– Afternoon game drive at 3:00 PM.
– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** in Serengeti.

title: Six-Day Northern Circuit Safari
description:
This itinerary covers Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Serengeti, and Ngorongoro, offering a comprehensive wildlife experience.

ItineraryDay 1– Depart from Arusha at 7:00 AM.– Arrive at Tarangire National Park by 9:00 AM for a game drive.– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** near Tarangire.

Day 2– Depart for Lake Manyara at 8:00 AM.– Game drive in Lake Manyara National Park.– Overnight at a **budget, midrange, or luxury lodge** near Manyara.

title: Nairobi City Tour
description:
Whether you are just beginning your game viewing experience in Kenya or preparing for your return trip home at the conclusion of your awe-inspiring travels in East Africa, Nairobi is a diverse and exciting urban setting for you to spend one or two days exploring the many attractions within the city limits. A guided city tour takes you to some of the most distinct attractions with a skilled and experienced guide that not only ensures your safety but also efficiently navigates the high traffic roads, allowing you to enjoy all that Nairobi has to offer.

Learn about African snakes and other animals at the Nairobi Snake Park and then visit the Nairobi National Museum where fossils from the East African river valleys are displayed, many of which tell the earliest stories of mankind. Your city tour also includes the capital’s shopping areas, local street markets, significant buildings, and other areas of interest.

The rich history of the metropolis is an integral part of what has become known as the “Nairobi character,” and your Wilber Safari guide shares the story of how swampland evolved into a thriving center of business and culture in Kenya, a transformation that began with the arrival of the railway in 1899. Explore the Wilber Safari experience to discover Nairobi’s vibrant history and cultural attractions.

title: Nairobi Snake Park
description:
Opened in January 1961, the Nairobi Snake Park is a distinct herpetology attraction that works to promote and preserve the snakes and reptiles found in Kenya. The snake displays were initially located at the Nairobi National Museum entrance. Later, the museum trustees acquired land in front of the museum and along the Nairobi River, now serving as a botanical garden and site for live snake exhibits.

The Nairobi Snake Park, now part of the Wilber Safari experience, is not only a curious public attraction but also provides a peaceful setting to appreciate Kenya’s unique urban-natural beauty. Alongside snake displays, Wilber Safari visitors can explore exhibits featuring marine and freshwater fish, reptiles, amphibians, birds, small mammals, and various invertebrates. Species include the Mombasa train millipede, freshwater prawn, giant snail, crayfish, baboon spider, and more.

Beyond public displays, the conservation park, under the Wilber Safari umbrella, leads efforts to protect and preserve creatures through initiatives like a rescue and rehabilitation program for abandoned or illegally collected reptiles. Community-based programs encompass education on aquarium fish and reptiles, snake removal from homes, snake bite prevention education, and snake identification services. Explore the Wilber Safari experience to learn more about the Nairobi Snake Park’s conservation efforts and diverse wildlife displays

title: Giraffe Center
description:
The Giraffe Center was first opened in response to the endangered status of the Rothschild giraffe, the population of which had declined, reaching a low of 130 animals in the wild as a result of agricultural practices in their native habitat. Successful fundraising efforts secured monies to move four herds of these graceful animals to Lake Nakuru National Park, Nasalot Game Reserve, Mwea Game Reserve and Ruma National Park, and the success of these efforts has increased the population to over 500 individual giraffes in Kenya alone.

The most exciting part of your tour visit to the Giraffe Center is the experience of seeing these animals up close and even feeding them! To raise awareness, the center provides free environmental education to young Kenyans and promotes conservation efforts of endangered species in Kenya. The Giraffe Center is open daily and the majority of your entrance fee goes toward supporting the important efforts of the organization. For those who want an up, close and personal wildlife experiences with Rothschild giraffes, Dawn In Africa Safaris recommends staying at the premier Giraffe Manor, a distinct country home adjacent to the center.

title: Animal Orphanage
description:
The David & Daphne Sheldrick Elephant Orphanage and Animal Orphanage plays a significant role in both local and international conservation efforts. Established in 1977, the trust is entirely supported by donations and memorializes the work of David Sheldrick, a renowned naturalist who served as the founder warden of the Tsavo East National Park in south-eastern Kenya from 1948 to 1976.

When you visit the orphanage during your Dawn in Africa tour, you will see orphan elephants and rhinos nurturing and learn about the trust’s two major programs – Orphan’s Project and Fostering Program. The Orphan’s Project is a globally recognized program that rescues and rehabilitates elephants and rhinos that have been orphaned due to poaching and habitat loss, and hundreds of infant elephants have been hand-raised and reintroduced to the wild at Tsavo East.

The Fostering Program, in partnership with the Kenya Wildlife Service, supports an elephant nursery in Nairobi National Park that is overseen by Dr. Dame Daphne Sheldrick who is credited with developing a complex milk formulary, as well as an in-depth understanding of husbandry.

title: Nairobi National Park
description:
Covering more than 45 square miles (117 sq. km) of grasslands, forests, rocky valleys, forests and wetlands is Nairobi National Park, Kenya’s oldest national park and the only one in the world that is situated amidst a capital city. The proximity to an urban center, though, does not diminish the wildlife presence within the park boundaries, though, as more than 100 species of mammal and over 500 types of avi-fauna are found in the park including 20 seasonal migrants from Europe.

What makes Nairobi Park surreal when compared to your other safari destinations in East Africa is that it is not uncommon to see grazing herbivores, such as giraffe, wildebeest, eland, Thomson’s gazelle and many others grazing quietly with the distant high-rises of Nairobi as a backdrop.

No other place in the world can you photograph free-roaming leopard, lion, buffalo, rhino and other animals in its natural ecology against a city skyline (read more on where to stay in Nairobi National Park). The park has both natural and man-made boundaries, with fences set on all but the southern edge, an open space that is used by migratory animals when new vegetation and water sources develop outside the park.

title: Half Day Mombasa City Tour
description:
The second largest city in Kenya, which is on the island, diverse and intriguing population, women in dark bui-bui or wrapped in brilliant multicolored kanga, a visit to Fort Jesus, the old town, Baobab forest, Elephant Tusks and Mama Ngina Drive (Baobab Garden).

The diversity of Mombasa is remarkable, making the city unlike any other in Kenya, with a unique cultural blend of Africans, Arabs and Asians representing many religious belief systems, including Christian, Muslim, Buddhist and Hindu. Touring the city not only allows you to see the architecture and learn about the cultures, but you also gain a deep understanding of the historical foundation of the ethnic diversity that makes Mombasa one of the most interesting destinations in Africa, such as how Afro-Asians and Afro-Arabs including the Swahilis have been living on the coast of East Africa for more than 500 years.

Stops on the trip include local bazaars, Fort Jesus, visits to the handicraft carvers and the Mackinnon Market. The first stop on the excursion, Fort Jesus, was built in 1593 based on designs by an Italian architect and engineer named Joao Batista Cairato. Originally used by the Portuguese, the imposing structure rests upon a coral ridge at the entrance of the old town harbor, and its strategic location has resulted in many battles for its control.

title: Kisite Mpunguti Marine Park
description:
Kisite Mpunguti Marine Park, located on the south coast of Mombasa near Wasini Island, is a protected marine haven encompassing coral reefs and four small islands. This park combines the Kisite Marine National Park and Mpunguti Marine National Reserve, covering a significant area. It features diverse wildlife, including over 45 coral types and 360 fish species, such as grouper, pufferfish, and dolphins.

Humpback whales and whale sharks are also occasionally spotted. The coral gardens beneath the water’s surface invite visitors to explore through snorkeling and scuba diving year-round. The Wasini Island mangrove boardwalk, managed by the Wasini Women Group, offers a chance to learn about the ecosystem. Additionally, visitors can shop for souvenirs and enjoy a seafood lunch at Charlie Claws Restaurant.

Afterward, exploring the Shimoni Slave Cave on the mainland reveals a haunting historical testament to the African slave trade, with chains and metal studs still lining the cave walls where captives were held before being transported to various parts of the world.

title: One Day Tsavo East
description:
Explore the captivating Tsavo East National Park on an authentic East African wilderness adventure. This biodiverse gem, part of the vast Tsavo National Parks, showcases 4,535 square miles (11,747 square kilometers) of untamed beauty. Accessible from Mombasa’s coast, the park unfolds before you with sweeping savannahs, acacia-dotted plains, and intriguing geological wonders like the Yatta Plateau and Mudanda Rock.

Follow the meandering Athi River to witness Lugards Falls and its resident crocodiles, and discover traces of human history amidst the landscape. Tsavo East is a haven for bird enthusiasts with over 500 species, making it an ideal Kenya birding destination.

Join Owira Tours & Safaris for an unforgettable wildlife-focused safari in this remarkable wilderness, easily reachable from coastal resorts like Mombasa, Malindi, Watamu, and Kilifi. We’re excited to craft an extraordinary African adventure that surpasses your expectations.

title: Tamarind Dhow Dinner Cruise
description:
Traditionally used for trade on the Indian Ocean, the Arab dhow has been a long-standing presence on the East African coastal waters, and now you can enjoy an evening of sailing, dining and romantically dancing along the coastline aboard one of these historic sailing vessels. Built in 1977, the Nawalilkher, an authentic Swahili dhow, is the largest of its type on the Mombasa coast, measuring more than 75 feet long.

Your evening cruise begins at the Tamarind Pier, where you board this magnificent wooden boat, and you feel as if you are stepping back in time. A kind waiter in traditional garb greets you warmly and escorts you to a private table where you sip on a house cocktail made with vodka, lime, honey, sugar and crushed ice, or if you prefer, you may also order your favorite non-alcoholic beverage or the cocktail without the alcohol.

As the dhow leaves the pier at around sunset, the setting could not be more delightful. The dhow sails around Tudor Creek where you enjoy breathtaking views of Old Town and Fort Jesus before arriving at a sheltered bay where the feast begins with an initiation of a live band playing! A four-course meal of mouth-watering edible delights is served, such as grilled lobster, fish, steak and much more!

title: Shimba Hills Full Day
description:
Depart early in the morning from your hotel at 06:00 am to Shimba Hills National Reserves .Arrive at the gate and start game watch then visit Sheldrick water falls escorted by a ranger, this is a nature walk in style. Shimba hills national reserve is a small park in the coast province, It cover an area of 220 km2 and gazetted in 1968.The reserve is an area of coastal rainforest, woodland, grass land and tropical.

The rare and endangered Sable Antelopes – 1 Day Shimba Hills Safari It is an important area for plant biodiversity over 50% of the rare plants are found in Shimba hills, including some endangered species of cycad and orchids. It is also a national important site for birds, over (420 bird’s species) and butterflies.There are estimated to be approximately 700 elephants in the reserve, this population is un stainable high because it causes significant damage to the vegetation threatening the endangered plant life. Conflict between human and elephants has also reach critical level .Other animal including: sable & Roan antelopes, Giraffes, Hartebeests, impalas, warthog colobus monkeys, baboons, Buffalos and rare Leopards.

Drive to the Shimba lodge for lunch. (The only tree top hotel in the coast). Watch the animal at the water hole. Proceed on with the game drive after lunch and depart back to your beach Holiday hotel .Arrive early evening

title: 2 Days 1 Night Tanzania Safari
description:
Day 1 Arusha – Tarangire National ParkIn the morning you will start your safari with a drive to the Tarangire National Park with packed lunch. The park, roughly the size of Luxemburg, covers an area of approximately 2,600 km². The landscape of savannah, seasonal swamps, and giant baobab trees is home to Masai giraffes, buffaloes, hartebeest, eland, fringe-eared oryx, the weird-looking long-necked gerenuk and over 550 species of birds. But it is the elephants, Tarangire is famous for and the park, where herds of up to 300 animals roam, is probably one of the best places in East Africa to see them and to get close to these giant creatures. After an extensive game drive, then drive to Kizumba Camp Site for dinner and overnight.

Day 2 Ngorongoro Crater – ArushaAfter breakfast drive to Ngorongoro and descend into the crater with packed lunch. Inside the world’s largest caldera, you will see a diversity of wildlife ranging from black rhinos, Grant’s and Thomson’s gazelles, lions and leopards to elephants and mountain reedbucks in the forests on the slopes. You might also see buffaloes, spotted hyenas, rare wild dogs and some of the more than 500 bird species of the area. After an intensive game drive and a picnic lunch, you will leave the caldera in the afternoon and travel back to Arusha where you will arrive in the early evening.USD 430 per person.

title: 3 Days 2 Nights Safari Tanzania Safari
description: Day 1 Arusha – Tarangire National ParkIn the morning you will start your safari with a drive to the Tarangire National Park with packed lunch. The park, roughly the size of Luxemburg, covers an area of approximately 2,600 km². The landscape of savannah, seasonal swamps, and giant baobab trees is home to Masai giraffes, buffaloes, hartebeest, eland, fringe-eared oryx, the weird-looking long-necked gerenuk and over 550 species of birds. But it is the elephants, Tarangire is famous for and the park, where herds of up to 300 animals roam, is probably one of the best places in East Africa to see them and to get close to these giant creatures. After an extensive game drive, then drive to Kizumba Camp Site for dinner and overnight.Day 2 Ngorongoro CraterAfter breakfast drive to Ngorongoro and descend into the crater with packed lunch. Inside the world’s largest caldera, you will see a diversity of wildlife ranging from black rhinos, Grant’s and Thomson’s gazelles, lions and leopards to elephants and mountain reedbucks in the forests on the slopes. You might also see buffaloes, spotted hyenas, rare wild dogs and some of the more than 500 bird species of the area. After an intensive game drive and a picnic lunch, you will leave the caldera in the afternoon and travel toKizumba Camp Site (near Manyara Park) for dinner and overnight.Day 3 Lake Manyara National Park – ArushaAfter breakfast, you will drive to the smaller Lake Manyara National Park with packed lunch. The park is known for its over 400 bird species, primate-filled forests and grassy plains. A large area of the park is covered by the alkaline Lake Manyara, which is home to colonies of flamingos and other water birds. Apart from elephants, you might also spot giraffes, hippos, wildebeests, impalas and teeming groups of noisy monkeys and baboons in the forests near the lake. In the afternoon, after an extensive game drive, you will drive back to Arusha.USD 585 per person.

title: 4 Days 3 Night Safari Tanzania Safari
description:
Day 1 Arusha – Tarangire National ParkIn the morning you will start your safari with a drive to the Tarangire National Park with packed lunch. The park, roughly the size of Luxemburg, covers an area of approximately 2,600 km². The landscape of savannah, seasonal swamps and giant baobab trees is home to Masai giraffes, buffaloes, hartebeest, eland, fringe-eared oryx, the weird-looking long-necked gerenuk and over 550 species of birds. But it is the elephants, Tarangire is famous for and the park, where herds of up to 300 animals roam, is probably one of the best places in East Africa to see them and to get close to these giant creatures. After an extensive game drive, then drive to Kizumba Camp Site for dinner and overnight.

Day 2 Serengeti National ParkAfter breakfast drive to Serengeti National Park with packed lunch, while traveling through the Ngorongoro Conservation Area you might stop for a breath-taking view over the Ngorongoro Crater before continuing to the Serengeti National Park. Serengeti means “endless plains” in the language of the Maasai and you will see the grassland savannah stretching to the horizon. Bizarre rock formations called kopjes dot the grassy landscape where predators are often seen. Depending on the season you will witness the migration of thousands of animals for which this park is famous for. You will arrive at your campsite for dinner and overnight in the Seronera Valley in the central Serengeti in the late afternoon after a game drive through the central Serengeti.

Day 3 Serengeti National Park – Ngorongoro Conservation AreaAfter breakfast you join another game drive in the Serengeti National Park. This morning you might spot animals you haven’t seen yet. The Serengeti is also home to the Big Five – lion, elephant, buffalo, rhino and leopard – and today you might be able to see all the members of this group. After lunch at a picnic site, you will drive in direction of the Ngorongoro Crater. As soon as you have reached the edge of the crater, the caldera opens up before you. Over 25,000 animals live in this approximately 20 km wide crater. You will spend the night and dinner at a Simba Campsite directly on the crater’s edge with fantastic views over the caldera.
